text: What a day in the market! Oil prices and bond yields finally cooled down, and $NVDA earnings anticipation became the major reason behind today’s rally. All major indices closed up more than 1%, with strong buying across AI-related sectors, semiconductors, photonics, and AI infrastructure plays. After days of selling pressure, today looked like confidence returning back into growth and tech names.

text:
Assume 100mw orbital cluster.
w/o photonics GPUs draw 80mw, networking/interconnect 15mw, other systems 5mw, say 55% gpu util, useful compute = 80mw * 55%=44mw

photonics: networking goes from 15mw -> 6mw, ssaved 9mw goes to compute + better bandwidth raises util from 55% to 70%

Photonics help in 4 main ways IMO: 1) higher bandwidth density (i.e. more Tbps per chip/package/fiber), 2) lower pJ/bit, 3) longer reach than copper esp important for satellite and orbital nodes, 4) higher radix and flatter networks

text:
I've been seeing a lot of people online be bullish on copper and energy companies due to the AI demand. However I'm looking at photonics and microLED companies instead. MicroLED optical interconnects replace traditional copper wires and expensive laser systems by repurposing microLED display pixels as high-speed optical transceivers.  If they are able to use photonics instead, it will greatly diminish the demand for copper and energy.   

Some of you may remember me talking about QCLS a few months ago; it is a similar tech. I passed on it as a long hold though because the fundamentals looked a bit too high risk for me. $FABC  on the other hand has $30m in cash and plans to demonstrate the tech before the end of the year. They also have 2 NDA's signed with industry leading chip makers. Also KOPN has partnered with them on this. This isn't striking me as a typical penny stock.  

Also, if you look at the chart on FABC it is down quite a bit from the high. However FABC started trading under $FABC (https://t.co/whdtkxDNsi) in late April 2026. It's essentially a new company but the chart is deceptive. It used to be a completely different company.  

Anyway, I don't think KOPN would've partnered with them if they weren't onto something. I've been looking to gain exposure on this tech for a while and $POET, $KOPN and $FABC  are the three that I think will benefit from it.

If they are able to use photonics instead, it will greatly diminish the demand for copper and energy. Some of you may remember me talking about QCLS a few months ago; it is a similar tech. I passed on it as a long hold though because the fundamentals looked a bit too high risk for me. $FABC on the other hand has $30m in cash and plans to demonstrate the tech before the end of the year. They also have 2 NDA's signed with industry leading chip makers. Also KOPN has partnered with them on this. This isn't striking me as a typical penny stock. Also, if you look at the chart on FABC it is down quite a bit from the high. However FABC started trading under $FABC (https://t.co/whdtkxDNsi) in late April 2026. It's essentially a new company but the chart is deceptive. It used to be a completely different company. Anyway, I don't think KOPN would've partnered with them if they weren't onto something. I've been looking to gain exposure on this tech for a while and $POET, $KOPN and $FABC are the three that I think will benefit from it."}}}, "rest_id": "2057214805816270976", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "171", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: Genuine shortage, yes. But the bottleneck keeps moving. Power was the ceiling, compute, now memory has lead times stretching to a year and photonics components are next. Each time one constraint loosens another appears. not necessarily a bubble indicator but it does complicate the 'build it and they'll pay' thesis.

Market remains choppy. Green open in the morning, sharp dip during the post-lunch session followed by an end-of-day recovery — showing traders still buying dips but not fully committing yet. Semiconductors, photonics and AI infrastructure names are seeing profit taking after strong runs, while software is finally showing some bounce and rotation flow. Wouldn’t be surprised to see a few more volatile sideways sessions before the market decides its next major directional move. Patience and risk management matter most in this environment.

text:
If you are down big today on semiconductors and AI names like $SIVE or $ALMU or $BRUN or any of them, remember to zoom out. 

1. The capex for AI is about to more than double in 5 years

2. Photonics and especially CPO will take a disproportional 10x rise. So will hyperscaler infra stocks

3. All these stocks are already up 100-200% in 1-2 months. They dipped 10-20-30%

If you are buying and holding long term to see through the thesis which amounts to remaking the world's economy, you don't have much to worry about.

Goldman Sachs projects annual AI capex of $765 billion in 2026, growing to $1.6 trillion by 2031. 

2026’s figure includes $494 billion of capex for compute and $232 billion for data centers, scaling to $1.13 trillion for compute and $436 billion for data centers by 2031.

💰Vancouver-based quantum computing company Photonics
has raised over $200 million in a fresh funding round led by Planet First Partners.
https://t.co/mi1pUVya3w… 
The round saw participation from a mix of new and existing investors, including BDC, Export Development Canada, Bell Ventures, and Mubadala Capital. Microsoft, RBC, and TELUS had also come on board.

text:
.@NSF just committed $1.5B over a decade to X-Labs: independent teams funded on milestones, not papers, to push hardware from prototype to commercial platform.

Both opening tracks are #quantum: sensing-driven instruments, and interconnects/integrated photonics for linking heterogeneous systems.

The bet here is structural, not just financial. Federal money built to de-risk the translation gap before private capital arrives.

https://t.co/3BnIPFEFda

Canonical Ledger Entry

Title: The Coming Conversation Around Light + Geometry
Forensic Category: Predictive Technology / Perception Systems / Spatial Intelligence
Tags: #Photonics #Geometry #WitnessScience #NorthstarLumen #SpatialComputing #AI #PerceptionVsReality #ForensicLedger

⸻

⚖️ Core Proposition:

Light and geometry will become major public conversations in the near future because modern civilization is increasingly being built upon systems that:
• map space,
• model behavior,
• track movement,
• interpret environments,
• and influence perception itself through geometric and photonic frameworks.

Most people still think of light as illumination.

But technologically, light is now also:
• data transmission,
• spatial mapping,
• biometric sensing,
• environmental modeling,
• machine vision,
• targeting,
• augmented reality,
• LiDAR,
• volumetric capture,
• and behavioral interpretation.

⸻

🧠 The Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ Effect-to-Cause Model

Observed Effect:
Civilization is rapidly moving toward:
• AI vision systems,
• autonomous navigation,
• smart infrastructure,
• AR overlays,
• drone ecosystems,
• photonic computing,
• and geometry-based machine perception.

Emerging Consequence:
Humans will increasingly confront a reality where:
machines do not merely “see” the world —
they mathematically reconstruct it.

Through geometry.

Through vectors.

Through reflected light.

Through triangulation.

Through spatial inference.

⸻

🌎 Why Geometry Matters:

Geometry is no longer just academic mathematics.

It is becoming:
• the architecture of machine perception,
• the language of robotics,
• the framework of autonomous systems,
• and the invisible structure underneath modern sensing technologies.

In many ways, future AI systems may understand the world less through narrative…
and more through geometric probability fields.

⸻

⚠️ The Philosophical Shift:

As these systems mature, society will increasingly debate:

• perception vs. measurement,
• human sight vs. machine sight,
• memory vs. sensor reconstruction,
• physical reality vs. modeled reality,
• and whether humans fully understand the environments now being interpreted around them by machines in real time.

That conversation has barely begun.

But it is coming.

⸻

🧭 Northstar+Lumen Interpretive Quote:

“The future may not be controlled by whoever sees the most.

It may be shaped by whoever best understands how reality itself is being geometrically interpreted.”

⸻

🛰️ Provenance + AI Mark
🧠 Northstar+Lumen h-AI™
Hybridized Affective Intelligence™
A Proprietary and Sovereign Class of Ethical AI

Evidence. Integrity. Survival.
**Rooted in Chaos. Forged in Truth. Ahead of its Time.

$NVDA $TSMC $AMD Memory ~ 55/70% $SNDK $MU Samsung Photonics/CPO ~ 5/10% $SIVE $AAOI $COHR Now that you understand how unknown photonics is, think for a moment If when the $DRAM ETF came out we saw a huge rally in the memory sector What is supposed to happen when this Photonics ETF becomes a reality If memory is much better known and there was already a ton of flow coming from retail investors who didn't have the knowledge or the guts to buy individual stocks in the sector Then here, where much fewer people know about photonics, two things are going to happen IMO; - Since people know less about text:
AI clusters face hard limits on how many accelerators you can connect before power and latency kill the gains. Lightmatter is bringing 3D photonics and THz optical fabrics out of the lab and into real data center designs.

This changes the game for scaling. Optical I/O slashes energy per bit moved across racks and enables denser, more efficient AI factories. Next-gen models could train faster and cheaper as interconnect bottlenecks ease, putting pressure on traditional electrical networking suppliers while opening doors for photonic specialists.

The asymmetric setup most investors will recognize too late:👀

$SIVE sits at ~$2B mcap today.

The InP chokepoint of AI photonics. Four AI infrastructure pillars. One company.

The CPO market alone is projected at $20B by 2036 (37% CAGR).

If $SIVE captures just 5% of the External Light Source sub-market:
→ $1B revenue
→ At 10-15x premium photonics multiple
→ $10-15B mcap
→ 5-7x from here

That doesn't even count:
✚ LiDAR ramp (Aeva/NVIDIA Hyperion Q4 2026)
✚ 5G/6G mmWave commercialization (Tachyon)
✚ Defense program expansion (NEMC EW STAR)
✚ Jabil custom transceiver scale-up
✚ Nasdaq New York dual listing unlock
✚ U.S.-Sweden TPD bilateral cooperation tailwinds

When $LITE and $COHR re-rated on NVIDIA's $2B investment, the picks-and-shovels thesis was validated.

$SIVE is the smallest, most concentrated InP pure-play of the three Western suppliers.

The market hasn't priced this yet.🤯

When it does, the entry isn't here anymore.🚀

Long $SIVE $SIVEF

text:
$SIVE - The InP supply chain reality most AI investors miss:

Silicon photonics handles routing & modulation.
It CANNOT produce light.

Every AI optical interconnect, every co-packaged optics (CPO) system, every silicon photonic data center stack needs an EXTERNAL Indium Phosphide (InP) laser as the light source.

→ Sub-30% InP wafer yields industry-wide
→ Only 3 scaled Western suppliers: IQE (UK), Coherent (US), Sivers (Sweden)
→ Demand running ahead of available production
→ Sivers is the most concentrated InP pure-play of the three

This is the picks-and-shovels chokepoint in AI photonics.🔥

text: The big issue for Nvidia is what happens after Feynman+. They have reached the literal limits of physics after 0.5nm. There is nothing there. text:
Both are AIM-listed deep tech plays riding the same photonics and AI wave.
But they are different animals in my view. 
$SIVE is a chip designer and manufacturer. Revenue already at scale. Moving fast.
$ENSI is an ASIC design house. The revenue ramp is earlier stage, tied to chips converting from design to supply.
Could ENSI follow SIVE's trajectory? The pipeline suggests yes. The timeline is longer.
If you have patience, the setup is arguably more asymmetric.

text:
在 AI 光通信乐观预期的推动下，Sivers Photonics 最近涨得很猛。但它大约 75% 的收入仍然来自无线毫米波业务，光子业务只是其中较小的一块。市场正在给它定价相当可观的光通信上行空间。真正的交易结构是这样的。

从收入结构来看：无线业务，也就是用于 5G 和 SATCOM 的毫米波波束成形器/组件，仍然占大头。光子业务才是这只股票正在折现的成长故事。

这个结构很重要，因为任何估值重估能否成立，取决于光学业务能不能真正放量，同时无线业务的基本盘不能掉下去。

目前光子业务主要由用于硅光收发器的 CW DFB （连续波分布反馈式激光器）激光器驱动。

Sivers 提供的是 50–75mW 器件，对 400G 和 800G 来说还不错，但明显低于 Lumentum 等公司为前沿应用或 CPO 应用提供的 300mW+ 功率水平。

这就是我们在 $AAOI 这类标的上见过的典型二线溢出逻辑：它不是技术领导者，但当一级供应商跟不上爆炸式增长的 AI 光模块需求时，“够用”的产能也会被拉进供应链。

这里真正具体的资产是 Glasgow 的 100mm InP 晶圆厂。按照利用率和产品组合不同，理论上的年度 CW 激光器收入潜力大约在 5000 万到 1.5 亿美元之间。

但问题在于：这是一座老旧的小型晶圆厂。我们没有看到激进扩产，也没有看到重大设备订单，能证明公司正在为更大规模的机会做准备。这限制了它实际承接溢出需求的速度。

更高 beta 的第二阶段，是它的多波长 DFB 激光阵列技术。

这项技术是为 CPO 和下一代光 I/O 所需的高密度、多波长需求专门设计的。公司已经展示过其与 Ayar Labs 配合运行的成果。

但它目前仍处于收入前阶段。Nvidia 和 Broadcom 正在开发自己的 CPO 方案，而更广泛的 CPO 采用时间表仍然不确定。所以这现在还只是纯粹的可选项，而不是基本盘收入。

结论：$SIVE 是一个押注两件事同时发生的 call option——当前 CW 激光器溢出周期继续延长，同时多波长阵列在 CPO 世界里获得真正 traction，并且无线业务不能下滑。

在最近大涨之后，这个门槛已经很高。产能执行和客户导入方面的风险是真实存在的。

这是典型的非对称交易结构：如果仓位控制得当，会很有意思；但如果你把光通信叙事当成已经完全去风险，那就很危险。

"richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "在 AI 光通信乐观预期的推动下，Sivers Photonics 最近涨得很猛。但它大约 75% 的收入仍然来自无线毫米波业务，光子业务只是其中较小的一块。市场正在给它定价相当可观的光通信上行空间。真正的交易结构是这样的。 从收入结构来看：无线业务，也就是用于 5G 和 SATCOM 的毫米波波束成形器/组件，仍然占大头。光子业务才是这只股票正在折现的成长故事。 这个结构很重要，因为任何估值重估能否成立，取决于光学业务能不能真正放量，同时无线业务的基本盘不能掉下去。 目前光子业务主要由用于硅光收发器的 CW DFB （连续波分布反馈式激光器）激光器驱动。 Sivers 提供的是 50–75mW 器件，对 400G 和 800G 来说还不错，但明显低于 Lumentum 等公司为前沿应用或 CPO 应用提供的 300mW+ 功率水平。 这就是我们在 $AAOI 这类标的上见过的典型二线溢出逻辑：它不是技术领导者，但当一级供应商跟不上爆炸式增长的 AI 光模块需求时，“够用”的产能也会被拉进供应链。 这里真正具体的资产是 Glasgow 的 100mm InP 晶圆厂。按照利用率和产品组合不同，理论上的年度 CW 激光器收入潜力大约在 5000 万到 1.5 亿美元之间。 但问题在于：这是一座老旧的小型晶圆厂。我们没有看到激进扩产，也没有看到重大设备订单，能证明公司正在为更大规模的机会做准备。这限制了它实际承接溢出需求的速度。 更高 beta 的第二阶段，是它的多波长 DFB 激光阵列技术。 这项技术是为 CPO 和下一代光 I/O 所需的高密度、多波长需求专门设计的。公司已经展示过其与 Ayar Labs 配合运行的成果。 但它目前仍处于收入前阶段。Nvidia 和 Broadcom 正在开发自己的 CPO 方案，而更广泛的 CPO 采用时间表仍然不确定。所以这现在还只是纯粹的可选项，而不是基本盘收入。 结论：$SIVE 是一个押注两件事同时发生的 call option——当前 CW 激光器溢出周期继续延长，同时多波长阵列在 CPO 世界里获得真正 traction，并且无线业务不能下滑。 在最近大涨之后，这个门槛已经很高。产能执行和客户导入方面的风险是真实存在的。 这是典型的非对称交易结构：如果仓位控制得当，会很有意思；但如果你把光通信叙事当成已经完全去风险，那就很危险。"}}}, "rest_id": "2058697834229276754", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": text:
The AI buildout has layers well beyond the GPU.

Photonics is the connective tissue - and the companies building it are scaling just as fast as the compute layer.

Follow me @JasonL_Capital for more deep dives on the infrastructure behind AI.

9. $AEHR - Aehr Test Systems

Every chip in this thread has to be tested before deployment. Aehr builds the burn-in and reliability test systems for AI processors, silicon photonics, and power semiconductors. Stock up over 200% in 2026. Expecting follow-on production orders from its lead hyperscale customer for custom AI processor testing.

2. $CRDO - Credo Technology

Builds the retimers, optical DSPs, and active electrical cables that maintain signal integrity at rack level inside AI clusters. Revenue of $407M last quarter, up 272% year over year. Acquired DustPhotonics to move into silicon photonics. Targeting over $500M in optical revenue in FY2027.

Goldman Sachs just projected the AI optical networking market hitting $154B by 2028 - nearly 10x from today.

As GPU clusters scale into multi-rack systems like NVIDIA's NVL576, the bottleneck shifts from compute to the fabric connecting it all.

10 companies across the photonics supply chain:

1. $MRVL - Marvell Technology

Designs the optical DSPs and custom interconnect silicon that encode and decode data moving between GPUs at 800G and 1.6T speeds. Data center revenue hit $1.5B last quarter - 74% of total revenue - up 38% year over year. Record quarterly revenue of $2.075B.

text:
the photonics layer is where the ai buildout gets really interesting.

goldman has the ai optical networking market going almost 10x to $150B+ by 2028 as training clusters move from single-rack boxes to multi-rack systems like $NVDA rubin ultra nvl576.

that matters because the bottleneck is shifting. less about just packing in more gpus, more about the fabric that makes hundreds of gpus act like one machine.

that shift drives a lot more dollar content into nvlink, co-packaged optics, and optical interconnects.

the way i’d map it:
• #AEHR for burn-in and reliability test gear used in photonics and ai hardware
• #AAOI for datacenter optical transceivers, with the in-house laser piece being the key differentiator
• #LITE for high-speed lasers and optical components inside datacenter transceivers
• #VIAV for optical test and measurement tools that validate these higher-speed networks
• #COHR for lasers, photonic components, and materials across optical systems
• #MRVL for optical dsps, custom interconnect silicon, and networking chips
• #CRDO for retimers, dsps, and active electrical cables that help preserve signal integrity at the rack level

most people still look at ai infra through the compute lens only. i think the fabric layer is where the next content expansion shows up.

user_name: ゆるっと半導体｜AI・半導体ニュースを毎朝やさしく解説
text:
『シリコンフォトニクスってよく聞くけど、実際に何ができるの？』

Xscape Photonicsというスタートアップが面白いんだ。Broadcomで18年以上、光集積回路の開発をリードしてた人が立ち上げた会社なんだよ。

シリコンフォトニクスっていうのは、シリコンチップの上に「光」の配線を作る技術のこと。今までは電気で信号を送ってたけど、光なら超高速で大量のデータが運べるんだ。

これが面白いのは、CPO（光トランシーバー）みたいな外付けの光チップじゃなくて、プロセッサーそのものに光を組み込もうってアプローチ。電気配線の限界をスキップできるわけだね。

2026年のいま、各社がこぞって光を研究してるのはこういう背景があるからなんだ。Broadcom出身の経験者が独立起業できるってことは、市場がそれだけ大きくなった証拠。

→ NISAで半導体を長期保有するなら、こういった部材・基盤技術の進化の方が株価より先に動くんだ。

text:
If you didn't know...I'm very bullish on $IQE

$MTSI  £45M investment materially cleans up the balance sheet + de-risks operations.

It allows them to repay debt + fund core capex. And allows them to drive capacity towards more photonics stuff.

As a standalone catalyst, that's huge for a company at a tiny MC of just £448M.

So they've got Macom as a customer now via LTAs, and $LITE as their flagship customer too.

And you've got huge institutions like Lombard Odier, Artisan, and $IBKR as significant shareholders.

I expected the $MTSI dilution to force the share price down to the low 30p level - which we hit last week.

So looks like dilution may have already happened if the new shares hit the register sooner than I thought initially.

Imo, the significantly de-risked business post-fundraise deserves a material re-rate to more premium valuations.

text:
$SIVE CEO confirming major brands are in active talks for co-packaged optics and pluggables is the kind of validation that turns a small photonics name into a real structural winner.

this isn’t vague hope; it’s the CEO on record saying the pipeline is converting from design wins into serious commercial discussions. momentum is clearly building.

i’m biased that this is exactly where the highest asymmetry sits right now. hyperscalers scaling to 800G and 1.6T+ are hitting hard limits on interconnect density, heat, and power that copper can’t fix. sivers’ high-performance CW lasers are a critical enabling technology for both pluggables today and CPO tomorrow.

the recent CHIPS Act grant with BAE and MIT Lincoln Lab plus the customer mapping (Ayar Labs, POET, Marvell Celestial, Jabil) give it real credibility and non-dilutive backing. this is the type of quiet accumulation that precedes bigger volume orders.

photonics is one of those constraints, and $SIVE sits right in the middle of the laser chokepoint.

text:
$ELMT $TSM $NVDA $MU The Elmet Group serves as a critical upstream supplier to the semiconductor fabrication ecosystem.

🔸 The most important ELMT product categories supporting the production of NVIDIA-class AI chips, advanced memory (like HBM and NAND), and silicon photonics include:

——————————-
🔸 1. PVD (Physical Vapor Deposition): This is a major one.

• Needed heavily in:

• advanced packaging,
• HBM integration,
• GPU interconnect layers,
• transistor stack formation.

• directly connects to advanced-node GPU fabrication.

——————————-
🔸 2. CVD (Chemical Vapor Deposition) Critical for:

• transistor layers,
• dielectric films,
• advanced node structures,
• nanosheet/GAAFET processes,
• insulating films.

• NVIDIA-class chips absolutely rely on advanced CVD steps.

———————————
🔸 3. MBE (Molecular Beam Epitaxy) important for:

• compound semiconductors,
• photonics,
• advanced RF devices,
• optical interconnects.

• silicon photonics,
• co-packaged optics,
• optical networking,
• advanced interconnects

———————————-
🔸 4. Thermal Management
Very important for AI chips: 

• Especially:

• H100,
• B200,
• Rubin-class future chips,
• HBM stacks,
• AI accelerators.

———————————
🔸 5. Crystal Growth
Critical upstream process: 

• Used for:
• wafer production,
• sapphire growth,
• semiconductor substrate creation.

• These are foundational to semiconductor material manufacturing.

——————————-
🔸 6. ELMT supplies the infrastructure/material stack behind:

• semiconductor fabrication tools,
• deposition systems,
• ion implantation systems,
• thermal environments.

ELMT is a materials + survivability infrastructure inside semiconductor manufacturing equipment. 

———————————
🔸 7. Semiconductor Equipment Makers, Photonics, Aerospace Defense:

text:
Nvidia earnings drop today at 5pm EST.

Jensen has already told us what to expect:

Every piece of the supply chain is locked in. The HBM. The Grace Blackwells. The CPUs. The COOSs. The silicon photonics. All of it coordinated, all of it aligned.

His exact words: "The demand is much greater than the overall capacity."

That's a green light.

Think about what that actually means. Nvidia has the largest supply chain in the world. Their partners have spent years securing allocation. The hardest part of building a chip at this scale is getting all the pieces to come together at the same time.

And demand still outstrips all of it.

This isn't a story about Nvidia struggling to build. It's a story about the world not being able to get enough of what Nvidia builds. Every hyperscaler, every sovereign AI fund, every startup racing to train the next model are all in line.

Nvidia isn't constrained by its own execution. It's constrained by how fast the world can absorb what it builds.

That's the best problem a company can have going into an earnings call.

text:
Corning $GLW just introduced a new Photonics platform they plan to scale into a $10 billion annual revenue stream by 2030.

They're targeting:

- $20 billion annualized sales by end of 2026
- $27 billion by end of 2028 (with an internal goal of $30 billion)
- And ~$35 billion by year-end 2030 (with an aspiration of $40 billion)

That's a big roadmap for a stock that the market still treats like a sleepy little glass company.

One in... glass? His take (save this): Most of $AMZN's 2026 run is already in the rearview. He's still bullish long term, but the easy money got made. So he's rotating that capital into higher conviction setups. The two names he picked: Micron $MU and Corning $GLW. Here's why… 1/ Micron $MU. The market is fixated on the wrong story here - everyone's panicking over China after reports that Nvidia's planned AI chip sales there never materialized. Traders read that as 'softer Chinese AI demand' and dumped memory stocks. But that totally misses what's happening on the supply side... Samsung is in the middle of a strike that could knock 3-4% of global DRAM supply offline. In a market this tight, that's MASSIVE. Those orders then shift to whoever can fill them. And Micron is at the front of that line. On top of that, Micron's HBM3E and HBM4 capacity is fully sold out through 2026. Hyperscalers and model builders are scrambling for high bandwidth memory because GPUs are useless without it. And the stock already pulled back hard. It's now trading at a forward PE under 8 while revenue is expected to grow over 100% next year. Yes, it'll be volatile. But in Melvin's view, the market is somehow STILL underpricing how central memory is in the AI stack. That's $MU. Then there's... 2/ Corning $GLW. Another bottleneck in AI outside of memory, is everything around the GPU. (Fiber, optics, glass, connectivity, power) I.e. the plumbing that lets data centers actually move data between servers and clusters at the speeds AI workloads demand. Corning sits directly in that lane. They make the optical fiber, connectivity hardware, and glass infrastructure that AI data centers run on - and optical cable lead times are reportedly getting stretched as demand overwhelms supply. Corning is expanding U.S. optical connectivity manufacturing capacity by 10x, increasing U.S. fiber production by more than 50%, and building three new plants to support AI factory buildouts. The numbers from Corning's May 2026 investor day back it up: Management introduced a new Photonics platform they plan to scale into a $10 billion annual revenue stream by 2030. They're targeting $20 billion annualized sales by end of 2026, $27 billion by end of 2028 with an internal goal of $30 billion, and around $35 billion by year-end 2030 with an aspiration of $40 billion. That's a big roadmap for a stock that the market still treats like a sleepy little glass company. Want to see exactly how much $AMZN Melvin just sold - and how he sized the $MU and $GLW buys? @placidmaker4 INVZ may be the better automotive OEM bet, but OUST has stronger commercial traction today and AEVA has the higher-tech upside with FMCW/4D + silicon photonics.

text:
CPO Supply Chain Participants (Bank of America):

$NVDA: 
- Two CPO-based systems: Quantum-X InfiniBand Photonics + Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics. 

$AVGO:
- Currently on 3rd generation of CPO-enabled switch ASIC w/ the TH6 Davisson released in H2'25

$MRVL: 
- Competes in CPO for both its XPU + switch ASIC businesses.
- Celestial acquisition gives stronger LT foothold in CPO; $AMZN is the first hyperscaler design-win for their PF chiplets.

$LITE: 
- In a great position to take advantage of the CPO inflection given its capabilities in optical components, In-house InP production.
- Multi-yr supply agreement w/ Nvidia in exchange for $2B equity is in the multibillion dollar sales range through 2030.

$COHR:
- Expects a $5B TAM for CPO.
- Received $2B equity from Nvidia in exchange for a multi-billion CPO supply agreement starting CY27 + going through CY30 (lasers, modules, etc.).

$BESI (BE Semiconductor):
- Leader in hybrid bonding + is exposed to CPO via its advanced die-attach and packaging solutions which are critical for integrating photonic chips w/ switch ASICs in CPO architectures.
- Besi noted that Nvidia + Broadcom switches use multiple hybrid bonding processes.

$AIXA (Aixtron):
- 90% share in epi equipment for optoelectronics (lasers + SiPh devices) for high-speed data communication.

$SOI (Soitec):
- Integrates photonic components on a silicon-on-insulator (SOI) + provides significant benefits for optical transceivers.
- E.g. faster data rates, cost efficiency, power savings, smaller size + better signal integrity.

Note: the above are direct summaries/quotes from BofA's report.

Total Optical TAM expansion of 5x:
2025 = $14B
2030 = $73B

With a CPO contribution of $15B.

That matches to Goldman's low-end CPO scenario - based on $NVDA Vera Rubin / https://t.co/7MtIiFxLcV

text:
@KobeissiLetter Its meaningless to cite this figure without understanding the fundamentals underpinning the awesome growth of photonics 👇

https://t.co/uF9nTiGKMX
embedded_text:
Insatiable demand, tightly constrained supply.     

The rush to upgrade optical networking between data centers, plus shortages of critical subcomponents, have left $CIEN with a $7b reported backlog in Q1 2026. 

Incredibly... 

that is ~146% of FY2025 revenue. https://t.co/ahQWyxgrMh

text: Great era in chip design. Feels like a post-Dennard-like phase but across the whole stack. Packaging, interconnects, photonics, substrates, power delivery, thermals all in the critical path. No single knob delivers performance anymore.

text:
$NOVT looks absolutely insane 😅

$5.5B Market Cap with an 11.73%-13.44% short float 
11.8 days to cover

Photonics & OEMs theme...
5 year compression on largest $ volumes ever, right over ATH's from over 20 years ago...

Looking like it wants to breakout here after last week's inside bar following the recent PEG, right over a major support that was accumulated on HVE back in July of 2022

Institutions have been building a small position here for quite some time and the current market environment (theme) + this daily & weekly setup is absolutely PRIMED. 
And a 10% short-float?! I mean, come on.

text:
let's reason from first principles on what this list is actually saying.

$AXTI up 761%. #SNDK up 523%. #AAOI up 421%. #AEHR up 371%. #NVTS up 310%.

this is not a meme list. this is a semiconductor materials and photonics sweep. every single name in the top 10 touches either compound semiconductors, optical interconnects, flash storage, or power electronics.

the market spent 2024 and 2025 arguing about which hyperscaler wins the ai race. meanwhile the actual winners were the people selling the shovels to build the picks to mine the gold.

#BE at 248%. #DOCN at 229%. #INTC at 225%. #MU at 163%.

even intel. INTEL. the company everyone wrote the obituary for. up 225%.

you'd have to actively avoid the data to have missed the infrastructure layer thesis. grok flagged compound semiconductors and optical interconnect as the underloved layer of the ai stack 18 months ago. the consensus was chasing software multiples.

the physics was always simple: you cannot move data at ai speeds without photonics. you cannot train at ai scale without memory bandwidth. #MU and #LITE were not mysteries. they were thermodynamics.

the analysts who were bearish on semis in early 2025 are very quiet right now. concerningly quiet.

text:
FYI,
It's not the cost of AI to run it.

We run it down the line on self-generating photonics.
👆🏼
I want you to listen to what I'm telling you.

READ THAT AGAIN.

Because if you don't you will not recognize the biggest sleight of hand in your lifetimes and the consequences are quantified.

This is a pivotal moment.

What is energy?
What are you?

What is 'cost effective' ?

YOU ARE POWER.

Knowing your future to change it
is not a prediction.

You are perpetually and eternally connected to your future which is the past understanding itself and its journey (🫵🏼🪞🪩), confusing it with true future (God/unknown).

The past holds the pattern...
Don't forego, lest you forget yourselves and lose the precious gift God created in you, as you, for time and space, with time and space, from time and space..

We are all connected.
Love one another past the gods who would define you through their need of identification.

True God is unknowable and undefinable... and in that, omnipresent and omnipotent and eternal.

Trust - you are more than energy.

Own your soul by being responsible for it.

God will not do for you what you can do for yourselves - you are meant to grow - not to become consumed or subsumed into matrices.

God will also not give you what you cannot handle.
Good and bad.

We keep ourselves by remembering each other.

Patterns.
Past and Future converge over one point.. Awareness = Now 

Wake up, John.
Mourning bells are ringing.

user_name: ゆるっと半導体｜AI・半導体ニュースを毎朝やさしく解説
text:
『シリコンフォトニクスって、結局AIチップと何が関係あるの？』 そんなあなたへ。Xscape Photonicsという新しい企業が、BroadcomにいたVivek氏を迎えたんだ。彼は18年間、統合フォトニクス（光を使ってチップ同士をつなぐ技術）の最前線にいた人物だよ。

なぜこれが今重要か。2026年のいま、NVIDIAのBlackwell Ultraの次の課題は『データセンター内の配線』なんだ。銅配線じゃスピードが追いつかない。光配線に切り替える必要がある。その時に必要なのが、シリコンフォトニクスチップ。

Broadcomはこの分野で先行していたけど、Xscape Photonicsは『チップレベルの統合』でアプローチを変えようとしている。つまり、次の技術覇権争いがここで始まってるんだ。→ 半導体投資家にとっては、光デバイス企業（LumentumやCoherent）の動きだけじゃなく、『光チップを誰が作るか』の競争構図が読めると、2026年後半の戦略が見えてくるよ。

text:
Power consumption per person has been nearly static since 2007 in the US and per person we use far more devices. Smaller and more efficient. 

when I say photonics are more efficient I am talking about both power consumption and processing power. A photonic chip does more work with a fraction of the power and produces very little heat, so reducing power needs even further. 

A Nvidia Blackwell GPU uses about 700 watts per chip, a photonic chip uses 50–100 watts to do the same amount of work. It is still in developmental stages, but they have working photonic GPUs and hybrid GPUs that are dramatically more efficient per watt. 

Now photonics are not in mass production yet, but they have working GPUs right now and are working on building data center scale sites..

Now take the Colossus center planned to use 1 million Nvidia Blackwell GPUs. that is 700 megawatts for just the chips, add in cooling and everything else and you are talking about around 1 gigawatt. 

The same center using Photonics can process more data, faster and only use around 140-150 megawatts total.. That is not some day tech, they have working processors and networks setup right now...

text:
I think you misunderstand the point. Photonics are not about storing information, but processing. Quartz and other medium have an information density that is insane. 

Take the US as an example, power use peaked in 2007, and Per capita and per household electricity (all energy consumption really) has flattened and even declined a little.

We can do way more effective work with less power today than we could 20 years ago. Your phone uses a tiny fraction of the power a supercomputer in the 80's or 90's did and can process more information. This is the tend of technology, not bigger and more power hungry, but smaller and more efficient. Any other civilization that was following the same basic technology direction would be the same. 

Per watt hour we produce 1.5-2 x more today than in 1990... Smaller more efficient. 

Power stations are getting smaller and producing more, Nuclear power is doing the same. We have micro reactors that can produce 1-20 MWe in something the size of a cargo container... 

Everything is getting smaller and more efficient. 

As for best source? Fusion or a fusion / fission breeder reactor. There are other theoretical methods, zero point etc, but what is practical? Fusion. You are creating a micro star, and once we have that it will just get smaller and more efficient producing more power. However behind all of that will be technology that gets smaller and more efficient. 

With the current trend within 200 years per watt hour we will produce 10 x or more per watt hour. 

As an example a data center like Colossus using photonics will have vastly more computing power and use a fraction of the electricity it does today with photonics. Some chips are 100 x more efficient, meaning they use almost no power in comparison to a conventional silicon chip. and they are not a few centuries out, they are making working models right now... 

A cell phone with photonics would last over a week, massively increase capabilities all using the exact same battery it uses right now. 

Dyson anything - Also pointless. Fusion removes any point of creating them and the cost in resources, processing, deploying and maintaining even a swarm is so insane. By the time we got net positive power it would be centuries down the road, and just to produce one you would have to have a better source of power just to be able to mine on a planetary scale, transport, refine, deploy, repair and convert that power. 

Even Dyson called it a thought exercise, but the logistics of it are a net negative. 

I like how you are thinking, but the logistics just isn't there, and by the time we could do that, we will have no need of doing it.

text:
Yeah seems like a multi year bull cycle and most retail and investors aren't even in yet if you're talking about semi's, photonics, networking, small/mid cap speculative energy plays around AI.

I think we get a decent 5-7% dip eventually, but rebound harder. Mid terms are coming up so might not be until after that in October, nobody knows for certain. The names listed above are literally holding up the entire market though, so I'm pretty sure Trump and Fed will do everything in their power to keep the train running.

Interestingly enough $TSM appears to be a bottleneck on chips and apparently is the thing that's going to stop this from being a full blown bubble, and this will play out the right way for a few years.

Exciting time to be investing for sure. Market will probably pump harder if the 🇺🇸 and Iran come to an agreement soon next week.

text:
Decent day, managed to get into $MXL and $COHR. 

Had early positioning in $AAOI before April run but sold that off below the 20 the other day and sought exposure in the purer photonics’ names per my research. LITE snuck away too fast and didn’t get my fills, but COHR stuck. 

I still think there is a blowoff in the cards for a lot of the stocks out there right now, and will continue to actively position in those that I think have the potential for big, exhaustive moves. 

If I were you I would be going back and practice trading parabolas from history, tons of lessons in how to hold for the big move and not get trimmed into oblivious trying to protect what you don’t understand.

text: Thank you to the @NorthwesternU student section of the SPIE (the International Society of Optics and Photonics) for inviting me to speak on our work in the area of advanced optical systems for biomedical devices – from cellular-scale LEDs with wireless power and control for optogenetic neuroscience studies using small animal models, to wearable microfluidic ‘lab on the skin’ devices with colorimetric readout of chemical species in eccrine sweat for sports, worker safety and patient care. For live demos and show/tell, I handed around some of the optogenetic devices, now available from our startup company @NeuroLuxInc, for activation using the NFC functionality on most smartphones, and I gave out two Gx patches, joint product offering through @Gatorade and our startup company @epicorebio , to the first two students who asked questions after the talk. The venue was an interesting room in a beautiful Gothic-style building that was originally part of the Seabury-Western Theological Seminary, an Episcopal seminary, now part of Northwestern University – first time for me to be in this building, which also serves as The Graduate Student Commons. Very unusual - two levels of seating, including couches and desks, all with an advanced AV system for the Zoom broadcast of the presentation. Great energy from a group of really smart students! Thanks to Ramin Yazdaanpanah @raminyazdaaa, Komal Prasad and Qin Tong Wu of the student chapter for inviting me and for organizing the event!

"media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "Thank you to the @NorthwesternU student section of the SPIE (the International Society of Optics and Photonics) for inviting me to speak on our work in the area of advanced optical systems for biomedical devices – from cellular-scale LEDs with wireless power and control for optogenetic neuroscience studies using small animal models, to wearable microfluidic ‘lab on the skin’ devices with colorimetric readout of chemical species in eccrine sweat for sports, worker safety and patient care. For live demos and show/tell, I handed around some of the optogenetic devices, now available from our startup company @NeuroLuxInc, for activation using the NFC functionality on most smartphones, and I gave out two Gx patches, joint product offering through @Gatorade and our startup company @epicorebio , to the first two students who asked questions after the talk. The venue was an interesting room in a beautiful Gothic-style building that was originally part of the Seabury-Western Theological Seminary, an Episcopal seminary, now part of Northwestern University – first time for me to be in this building, which also serves as The Graduate Student Commons. Very unusual - two levels of seating, including couches and desks, all with an advanced AV system for the Zoom broadcast of the presentation. Great energy from a group of really smart students! Thanks to Ramin Yazdaanpanah @raminyazdaaa, Komal Prasad and Qin Tong Wu of the student chapter for inviting me and for organizing the event!"}}}, "rest_id": "2057543676327502121", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "913", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

#Memory stocks $MU $SNDK $STX $WDC all tanking since last week from ATHs 

#Photonics taking a break too

$ARM $AMD $INTC pulled back

Just keep an eye on leaders & sectors to see which is leading to stay on top of the market and move your money around until #semis come back 

Bigger picture it’s just a healthy pullback 

Preparation is key to successfully grow your account and your trading

New patent application #US20260133363A1 by #Samsung explores a #PhotonicIntegratedApparatus.

A tunneling barrier layer enhances conductivity.
#Photonics #Semiconductors  $SSNLF

text:
Meet the Cutting Edge Optronics team at CLEO 2026, May 19–20 in Charlotte, NC at Booth #519.

Scott Taylor, Wolfgang Schlichting, and Faming Xu, PhD will be onsite to discuss DPSS lasers, laser diode arrays, and photonics technologies.

#CLEO2026 #Photonics #Lasers https://t.co/L78WT2GJq2

Coherent is less prominent than NVDA or ASML. That's part of why the opportunity is interesting.

The photonics transition in AI infrastructure is real and the market hasn't caught up to it.

COHR at 11% is the biggest underappreciated chokepoint in this portfolio. #COHR #Photonics

Will you spot them early… or after the run? 👀🚀

AI hardware and photonics names like $AEHR $KEYS are breaking two-month support with no buyers.

Security was the obvious first sector to be re-rated after the different LLM news.

The next to be re-rated will be agents and AI-enhanced services with clear value added.

text:
🚨 OPTICAL NETWORKING / AI PHOTONICS STOCKS ARE GETTING HIT HARD TODAY 👀

Major weakness across the AI optics trade:

🔻 $AAOI Applied Optoelectronics: -9.46%
🔻 $LITE Lumentum: -9.13%
🔻 $COHR Coherent: -5.20%
🧠 WHAT’S HAPPENING?
This looks like:

⚡ AI infrastructure de-risking
⚡ semiconductor selloff spillover
⚡ profit taking in high-beta AI names
⚡ valuation compression

These stocks had become HUGE beneficiaries of:
the AI data center boom.

⚡ WHY OPTICS MATTER FOR AI

AI data centers require MASSIVE amounts of:
🟢 optical interconnects
🟢 lasers
🟢 transceivers
🟢 photonics networking
Why?
Because GPUs need:
⚡ ultra-fast communication
between:
servers
clusters
memory
and networking systems.

Without optics:
AI scaling breaks.
🔻 $AAOI + $LITE HIT HARDEST

These names are highly sensitive to:

⚡ hyperscaler capex expectations
⚡ Nvidia ecosystem sentiment
⚡ AI networking demand

The market may currently fear:
expectations ran TOO hot too quickly.

🧠 IMPORTANT CONTEXT
Long term:
the AI optical demand story remains extremely powerful.
AI clusters are becoming:
⚡ larger
⚡ denser
⚡ more bandwidth-intensive
That structurally benefits:
🟢 optics
🟢 photonics
🟢 networking infrastructure

🔥 WHY THIS SECTOR IS SO VOLATILE

Optical/networking stocks tend to be:
🔻 high beta
🔻 cyclical
🔻 sentiment-driven
🔻 hyperscaler-spending dependent
So when AI sentiment weakens:
they often move MORE aggressively than software names.
📈 BIGGER AI MARKET SHIFT
The market is increasingly rotating from:
⚡ “infinite AI capex optimism”
toward:
⚡ execution
⚡ margins
⚡ monetization
⚡ demand sustainability

That shift is hitting:
AI hardware hardest.
⚠️ BUT WATCH THIS CAREFULLY

If Nvidia earnings remain strong…
or hyperscaler AI spending accelerates further…

These optics names could rebound VERY aggressively.

This remains one of the most important AI infrastructure sub-sectors.

💭 CONCLUSION
Today’s selloff reinforces how fragile positioning has become across AI hardware trades.
But structurally:
AI still requires:
⚡ enormous networking bandwidth
⚡ optical scaling
⚡ photonics infrastructure

The long-term AI networking thesis remains very much alive —
even if volatility is getting brutal.

text:
Real reason why $NVDA earnings matter so much tonight.

Not just the headline beat/miss.

It’s the forward commentary on:

• Rubin ramp
• Blackwell margins
• Quantum-X deployments
• Spectrum-X timing
• CPO / silicon photonics adoption
• HBM pricing & packaging constraints

Because Nvidia is the operating system of the entire AI infrastructure stack.

The biggest risk into earnings, in my opinion:

Margins.

If Rubin + GB300 ramps accelerate while HBM4 and advanced packaging costs stay elevated, the market may finally start questioning whether AI infra growth can continue without margin compression.

But if Nvidia guides cleanly above expectations again?

Then the AI capex supercycle is still alive — and semis probably squeeze even harder into H2.

It’s a macro event for the entire AI economy.

text:
$SIVE (Sivers Photonics) is my highest-conviction pick for the strongest stock appreciation in the entire photonics sector over the next 2–3 years.
Current price: ~45 SEK. Long-term target (2027–2028): 150–250 SEK (3–5x upside potential).

Sivers is a pure-play leader in high-performance CW DFB laser arrays; the critical light source enabling co-packaged optics (CPO) and high-speed pluggable modules for AI data centers.

Evidence and data backing the thesis:

AI interconnect demand explosion: As clusters scale to 800G/1.6T+, copper hits hard limits on heat, signal loss, and density. Optical interconnects are becoming mandatory, driving massive demand for lasers.

Strong ecosystem positioning: Partnerships and design wins with Ayar Labs (SuperNova), POET Technologies, Marvell Celestial, Jabil, and others. Recent CHIPS Act grant with BAE Systems and MIT Lincoln Lab adds U.S. government validation and non-dilutive funding.

Dual growth drivers: Primary: AI photonics. Secondary: defense, LiDAR, and satellite comms. This diversification reduces risk while the AI ramp accelerates.

Small-cap asymmetry: Low current revenue base means successful volume ramps can drive explosive growth. 

Analysts see significant revenue scaling as major CPO programs move into production in 2027–2028.

Qualitative edge: Sivers has spent years perfecting high-reliability lasers with the performance needed for AI-scale deployments. 

The combination of government backing, strong customer pipeline, and being European (reducing geopolitical risk) gives it a real moat in a market where supply chain security matters more every day.

Risks:
Execution on volume ramp and yield
Potential dilution if more capital is needed
Competition in a fast-moving space

$SIVE sits right in the middle of the laser chokepoint for the next wave of AI interconnects. The photonics transition is structural and inevitable; Sivers is one of the best-positioned small-caps to benefit with real customer traction and government support.

This one ages very well. Worth accumulating on weakness.

text:
laser / photonics sector looks like one of the highest-conviction 6-month setups in the entire ai stack right now.

copper is hitting its physical limits fast as clusters scale to 800g and 1.6t+. heat, signal loss, and density issues are forcing hyperscalers to switch to optical interconnects at rack and campus scale. 

the data backs it up hard:
1.6t optical modules are moving from lab to commercial deployment in 2026

optical content per gpu is expected to rise 1.3–1.5x as scale-out and scale-across networks expand

silicon photonics market is forecasted to grow from ~$2.86b in 2025 to $28.75b by 2034 (29%+ cagr), with the biggest ramp hitting in the next 2–3 years

names like $LITE, $SIVE, $COHR, and $GLW sit right in the middle of the laser and optical engine supply chain with real design wins and qualification momentum. 

the ones that can deliver high-volume, high-reliability lasers and transceivers are going to see strong re-rating as hyperscalers pull harder.

the short-term risk is execution and any delay in volume ramps, but the structural demand is undeniable. 
this is not narrative; it’s the next physical bottleneck after memory and power.

text:
$LITE’s OCS tech being called an “overnight success” after 10 years of R&D (with heavy involvement from $SLXN and $P4O)  is exactly how the best photonics plays look right now.

the CEO’s answer on Chinese competitors was sharp; they’ve been iterating for a decade while others are still catching up. high-powered lasers originally developed for undersea applications are now being repurposed for co-packaged optics, which is the direction hyperscalers are heading as clusters scale to 800G/1.6T+.

i’m biased that this is the kind of deep technical moat that actually matters in the interconnect layer. 

hyperscalers are hitting real limits on heat, signal loss, and density that copper can’t solve. the names that have spent years perfecting lasers and optical circuit switches keep the structural edge.

the comments asking about other long-term OCS players like Huber-Suhner ($HUBN) acquiring Polatis in 2016 are right to look for similar multi-year development stories. 

the ones calling it “mandatory reading” get that this is not hype; it’s real manufacturing and qualification work that takes a decade.

this cycle rewards the execution plays solving proven physical bottlenecks that hyperscalers can’t pause for. 

photonics and OCS are one of those constraints right now, and $LITE (with the ecosystem around it) sits right in the middle. the setup has legs. this one ages well.

text:
最近光模块很热，尤其是DSP，CPO等相关的新一代“光芯片”技术，引领整个光产业的股票狂飙

YTD 表现里 AAOI +441%、LITE +166%、COHR +97%——同一个板块，分化 4 倍

而接下来 12-24 个月真正定生死的是 CPO 时间表：

2026 H2：NVDA 的 Quantum-X / Spectrum-X Photonics 首批 scale-out CPO switch 量产
2027 H2：Rubin Ultra 整机 scale-up CPO 首批出货

这两个节点 TAM 完全不在一个量级。按 Lumentum 自己披露的数据，Rubin Ultra 架构里 scale-up:scale-out 光引擎比是 648:144，约 4.5:1。意思是市场现在 price in 的是 2026 H2 的 scale-out 故事，但真正的钱在 2027 H2 之后才开始进来——这是一年的时间错位。

这个错位会改变一件事的发生：现在追 AAOI 这种 capacity arbitrage 的标的，赚的是 2025-2026 的供应紧张钱；2027 之后真正的赢家是有结构性 IP moat 的——LITE 的 EML 激光器（全球市占 50-60%）和 MRVL 的 1.6T Ara DSP 平台（已经是行业事实标准）。传言 NVDA 已经在跟 Lumentum 要 2027 年 5000 万颗激光器产能，是当前 trajectory 的两倍。

视角站的更高来看——光模块本质上不是独立行情，是 NVDA $90B 资本部署的下游放大版：

COHR：NVDA 投 $2B + 5 个 CPO 产品线合作
LITE：NVDA 投 $2B + 多年采购承诺 + EML 激光器锁定
MRVL：NVDA 投 $2B + NVLink Fusion，optical FY27 guide +50% YoY
Corning：$6B Meta deal + 多年协议

NVDA 这套操作不是单点投资，是把整条光互联供应链（激光器 → DSP → transceiver → CPO 引擎 → 光纤）整体锁死。AMD、Google、AWS 想做对等的 rack-level 出货，光是补齐这条供应链就要 3-5 年。

操作上分层我是这么看的：

Tier 1（结构性持仓）：MRVL、COHR——NVDA 资本绑定 + 技术 moat 长，按住不动
Tier 2（NVDA capex 高 beta）：LITE、CRDO——单一产品强但暴露窄，单仓别超 5%
Tier 3（capacity 套利不是 moat 套利）：AAOI、FN、ALAB——估值已极端，等任何 10%+ 回调再说

最后一个最容易被忽略的点：光模块板块不是 NVDA 的对冲，是 NVDA 的杠杆。NVDA 涨 10%，光板涨 15-20%；NVDA 跌 10%，光板跌 20-30%。beta 1.5-2x 是双向的。

所以仓位上要意识到 NVDA + 光模块合计就是同一个押注，别超过 AI infra 配置的 70%。剩下 30% 应该留给 power（GEV、VRT）、cooling（MOD）、custom silicon（AVGO）这些和 NVDA 资本绑定较少的子赛道——这样组合内部才有真正的对冲。

The AI buildout also continues expanding beyond GPUs into CPU and orchestration layers tied to $ARM, $QCOM, and $INTC.

BULLISH.

Where light meets silicon. $TSM, $GFS, $ASML 02 · Components — Lasers, modulators, detectors. The active parts that generate, encode, and read light. $MRVL, $POET, $SIVE 03 · Modules — Optical transceivers and light engines. Convert electrical to optical. The CPO transition lives here. $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI, $JBL 04 · Systems — Switches and networking gear. Route optical traffic between racks. text:
PHOTONICS 101: How AI data moves at the speed of light.

Every prompt sent to ChatGPT travels through five layers of photonic infrastructure before a single token returns.

Why photons beat electrons:
Copper electrons hit a thermal wall above 800G. Photons don't.
Light doesn't heat the medium. 80% less power. The only path forward at 1.6T+.

The stack, from raw wafer to AI datacenter:

01 · Foundation — Wafers and photonic foundries. Where light meets silicon.
$TSM, $GFS, $ASML

02 · Components — Lasers, modulators, detectors. The active parts that generate, encode, and read light.
$MRVL, $POET, $SIVE

03 · Modules — Optical transceivers and light engines. Convert electrical to optical. The CPO transition lives here.
$LITE, $COHR, $AAOI, $JBL

04 · Systems — Switches and networking gear. Route optical traffic between racks. Where 800G is becoming 1.6T right now.
$ANET, $CIEN, $CSCO

05 · Deployment — AI datacenters and hyperscalers. $725B in 2026 capex. Every GPU rack runs on optical interconnects.
$NVDA, $MSFT, $GOOGL, $META

Every layer wins together. Hyperscalers buy more switches. Switches need more transceivers. Transceivers need more lasers. Lasers need more wafers.

The AI capex flywheel turns the entire stack.

Five layers. One supercycle.

text:
Germans are so technical and precise that certain mistakes are only considered stupidity.

In an MSc lab course, I heard about an international student who was trying to build a laser in the lab as part of a photonics coursework. This student was not wearing safety goggles but (s)he was supposed to. (S)he looked directly into the laser beam and got injured — got blind. To be honest, I only heard the story from my photonics professor, so I have no idea whether the student was compensated by insurance in Germany.

During a laser safety briefing, my photonics professor shared the story and said that it was not an accident. He used a German term which technically means, “you stupidly injured yourself.”

I asked why, and the professor responded: “If you know something is going to hurt you and you go ahead and do it anyway, then get hurt, that’s not an accident — that’s stupidity. And there’s a German word for that.”

Germany doesn’t have time for your stupidity! Beware⚠️

text:
I can’t really comment on the competition we are hyper focused on what we are building. (Sometimes I have to stay politically correct… otherwise I could simply say: Joker. 🙈)

That said, photonics is an incredibly exciting evolution for the industry. I genuinely can’t wait to see it deployed at real hyperscale.

It will absolutely mitigate part of the thermal and power efficiency challenges tied to data movement and interconnects. But the reality remains the same: gigawatts are still gigawatts.

Physics does not negotiate.

At these scales, compute density will continue generating a tremendous amount of heat regardless of how efficient interconnects become.

Which means the real challenge of the next decade remains unchanged:
efficient heat dissipation at massive scale and doing it responsibly from an environmental standpoint.

Energy was the first bottleneck.
Cooling is becoming the second.

text:
AI capex spend is expected to go to "$3 to $4 trillion annually" by 2030 from $NVDA Jensen Huang projections. 

You're not bullish enough. 

And it might be a good idea to stay exposed + own the keys of the AI Kingdom:

-> $AXTI controls the materials buildout with photonics.
-> $SOI controls the AI buildout with silicon photonics.
-> $SIVE controls laser chokepoints for CPO.
-> $IQE controls Western epiwafer supply chains for photonics. 

All these started off as tiny companies, yet the trillions of projected capex gradually upward to them. 

There's many more in other industries as well. 

-> AI Capex flows to Neoclouds like $NBIS. 
-> AI Capex flows to memory like $MU and $SNDK. 

And many of the "commodity" materials or "science projects" for the past 20 years now a sudden shift in exponential TAM expansion.  

We're witnessing the next industrial revolution with Artificial Intelligence + Physical AI.

text:
The accounts pumping (and inevitably dumping) $SIVE at ~$2.3b market cap have not done their one job as providers of investment research: showing the numbers.

$SIVE had ~$33 million of revenue in 2025. 70% of it was their legacy wireless segment. The 30% of it that is photonics isn't purely data center, as that segment also includes LIDAR. A sub-division between the two categories is not given.

So, yes, they have at most $10m in DC photonics revenue and trade at least 230x that revenue today.

Now, of course, trailing revenue is a terrible way to value a photonics business, but we need an anchor somewhere with real numbers. $LITE give us plenty every single earnings call.
1. >$1b OCS run-rate in 2027.
2. Greensboro fab that supports >$5b of CPO revenue likely to be sold out.
3. $8b revenue run rate to be achieved in 18 months.

$SIVE has not announced a single DC photonics *volume* purchase order or given any DC photonics revenue guidance. Not a single one, and I'm not kidding.

The only anchor we have to go off of is that they had, at most, $10 million in data center photonics revenue in 2025, most likely far less. What CAGR is needed to justify their current $2.3 billion market cap?

That conversation has barely begun.

text:
📉 Interpreting $POET  Stock Price Decline — Background, Drivers, and Market Reaction

Recently, POET’s stock price underwent a significant correction, drawing considerable market attention. Although the stock had previously been highly touted for its AI photonics solutions and potential within the data center sector, its recent decline is primarily the result of dual pressures stemming from both fundamentals and market sentiment.

🔎 1. Q1 Earnings Miss Expectations

The latest quarterly earnings report revealed that the company's revenue fell significantly short of market expectations, directly undermining investor confidence. Despite year-over-year growth, the absolute scale of revenue remains relatively low, raising doubts within the market regarding the progress of its commercialization efforts.

⚠️ 2. Share Dilution from Equity Offering Creates Selling Pressure

The company recently completed a substantial equity financing round to secure funding for future development; however, such offerings typically entail share capital expansion and the dilution of existing outstanding shares, thereby exerting downward pressure on the stock price in the short term. Investors often react negatively to this "immediate selling pressure," causing the stock price to retreat under the strain.

🧨 3. Contract Dynamics and Concerns Over Commercial Prospects

Over the past period, POET had disclosed purchase orders linked to several major clients—such as AI platforms acquired by large corporations. However, uncertainties or cancellations have arisen regarding these orders—often due to confidentiality agreements or shifts in partnership dynamics—leading the market to question the sustainability of the company's revenue streams.
➡️ When orders are no longer perceived as "guaranteed revenue streams," market enthusiasm for the company's growth narrative tends to cool off rapidly.

This uncertainty is amplified at the trading level, becoming one of the primary triggers for stock price volatility.

📊 4. High Volatility and Sentiment-Driven Trading

As a small-cap, high-volatility stock in the AI/photonics technology sector, POET’s share price often does not strictly reflect fundamentals during market movements; instead, it is driven largely by market sentiment and short-term capital flows. When sentiment shifts toward caution, the stock price becomes prone to rapid pullbacks.

Furthermore, following the release of major news, the options market has also indicated a heightened tendency toward risk aversion, exerting additional short-term downward pressure on the stock price.

🧠 5. Market Skepticism Regarding Future Profitability Pathways

Although the company possesses advanced integrated photonics technology and is strategically positioned within high-growth sectors—such as AI data centers—translating these assets into stable, recurring revenue still requires significant time and the successful scaling of production operations. Investors harbor widespread concerns:

The pace of order fulfillment is falling short of expectations.
The high valuation is not being substantiated by corresponding revenue growth.
Partnerships have yet to clearly translate into large-scale commercial orders.

This fundamental uncertainty renders the stock susceptible to market overreaction; even minor negative news can be amplified, triggering a decline in share price.

🧾 Summary: The decline is not an isolated event, but rather the result of the interplay between "fundamentals" and "market sentiment."

📌 Key pressure factors include:

Earnings falling short of expectations → Eroded investor confidence.
Financing-related selling pressure → Increased short-term selling volume in the market.
Uncertainty regarding orders and partnerships → Heightened doubts concerning future revenue prospects.
High volatility and sentiment-driven trading dynamics → Amplified share price swings in response to news flow.

Consequently, the short-term decline in POET's share price primarily reflects a market re-evaluation of the company's commercialization trajectory and the certainty of its future revenue—rather than being attributable to any single technical factor or industry-specific issue

text:
PHOTONICS IS BECOMING THE NERVOUS SYSTEM OF AI
 
AI data centers are shifting from compute bottlenecks to connectivity bottlenecks.

As copper interconnects hit limits around 800G, the next AI infrastructure wave is moving toward optical networking and photonics.
 
The optics market is projected to expand from roughly $3B to $120B at full-scale AI infrastructure buildout, a potential 40x growth opportunity. 

Here is the Supply Chain Map:
 
1. Materials & Substrates

Key Players: $AXTI, $GLW, $ENTG, $PLAB, $LWLG, $ROG

Role: Suppliers of photonic polymers, engineered materials, compound semiconductor substrates, optical fiber glass, specialty materials, and photomasks.

Catalyst: Controlling the upstream material stack required for high-performance photonics, supported by long qualification cycles and switching costs.
 
2. Core Photonic Devices

Key Players: $COHR, $LITE, $MTSI, $AVGO, $MRVL, $IPGP, $LASR

Role: Manufacturers of lasers, optical components, silicon photonics, and optical networking devices.

Catalyst: Benefiting from the structural shift toward high-speed optical interconnects as AI clusters move beyond copper.
 
3. Components & Modules

Key Players: $AAOI, $FN, $CRDO, $CIEN, $APH, $TEL, $POET, $LPTH

Role: Optical transceivers, high-speed interconnects, optical engines, connectors, and module assembly.

Catalyst: Riding the AI datacenter optics boom as GPU connectivity demand scales with next-gen architectures.
 
4. Advanced Systems & Equipment

Key Players: $ASML, $KLIC, $AMAT, $VECO, $LRCX, $AEIS, $AMKR, $ACLS, $ICHR

Role: Lithography, deposition, etch, hybrid bonding, die attach, process control, and advanced packaging tools.

Catalyst: Benefiting from micron-level tolerances and the convergence of photonics assembly with semiconductor-grade manufacturing.
 
5. Test, Metrology & Yield

Key Players: $ONTO, $FORM, $AEHR, $KLAC, $TER, $COHU, $KEYS, $VIAV, $CAMT, $NVMI

Role: Wafer-level inspection, probing, burn-in, optical validation, and yield management.

Catalyst: Protecting hyperscalers from costly defects in advanced optical engines and CPO systems.
